channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Gov Otu Grants Automatic Jobs To Outstanding Corp Members
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Governor Bassey Otu, has granted automatic employment to seven indigenes of the state who distinguished themselves during the 2025 NYSC year and were honoured with the Presidential Honours Award by President Bola Ahmed Tinubu at the Aso Villa. The governor announced the package when he received the

item.title: NRS sets July 31 deadline for large taxpayers’ e-invoicing adoption
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The NRS has directed all large taxpayers to fully adopt the national e-invoicing and Electronic Fiscal System (EFS) on or before July 31, 2026. The post NRS sets July 31 deadline for large taxpayers’ e-invoicing adoption appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: World Cup Final Enters Extra Time After Spain, Argentina Stalemate
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final will be decided in extra time after Spain and Argentina played out a tense goalless draw at the end of 90 minutes at MetLife Stadium on Sunday. Neither side could find the breakthrough in a closely contested encounter despite creating promising openings, with both defen

item.description: Take a look at the key dates for the 2027 golf season, featuring the majors and Ryder Cup.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has said his commitment to investing in schools and hospitals stems from his conviction that both sectors are essential to improving lives and securing Nigeria’s future. Obi made the remark in a statement on Sunday

item.title: Rosy Meurer officially removes Churchill’s name from her bio amid marital crisis
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Rosy Meurer, the wife of Nigerian celebrity businessman Olakunle Churchill, has officially removed his name from her bio. This comes amid their ongoing marital controversy. It all started in January 2026 when Churchill reunited with his ex-wife, actress Tonto Dikeh, ahead of their son’s 10th b

item.title: Iran Executes 2 Men Convicted Of Setting Police Officers On Fire
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Iran’s judiciary on Sunday announced the execution of two men convicted over their roles in deadly violence during anti-government protests that peaked in January after erupting weeks earlier over worsening economic conditions. In a statement, the judiciary identified the men as Erfan Esfandia

item.title: Worker Rescued Alive As Wall Collapses During Demolition In Kano
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A worker was rescued alive after a wall collapsed on him during a demolition exercise at Kofar Mata Zango in Kano Municipal Local Government Area on Sunday. The Kano State Fire Service said it received a distress call at about 8:49 a.m. In a statement signed by the Service’s Public Relations O

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has called on other African countries to lend their voices in unanimously condemning the ongoing Afro-phobic attacks, also known as xenophobia, in South Africa. A united voice, according to the Nigerian leader, would send a clear message to that Southern African country that su

item.title: Korea President’s Stock Dream Bumps Into Leveraged ETF Backlash
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korea’s most closely watched stock-market innovation is morphing into a headache for President Lee Jae Myung.

item.title: Senegal’s President Faye elected new ECOWAS Chairman
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: His election comes at a pivotal moment for the 15-member regional bloc, which continues to grapple with terrorism, political instability, economic integration and efforts to redefine relations with Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger following their withdrawal from ECOWAS.

item.title: Tragedy hits Ooni’s family as elder brother dies suddenly
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Born on December 8, 1967, Prince Adetunji hailed from the illustrious Ojaja lineage of the Giesi Ruling House in Ile-Ife. He was the Group Chairman of Primewaterview Holdings and was widely respected in Nigeria's business community for his visionary leadership and entrepreneurial accomplishments.

item.description: A US service member died during a "controlled detonation" of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian drone in northern Iraq, the military said Sunday. It followed the killing of two US troops in Jordan late Friday. Jordan on Sunday summoned Iran's top envoy in Amman to protest Tehran's attacks on

item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) governorship candidate in Ogun State, Oladipupo Adebutu, has alleged that all state governors in Nigeria are benefiting from and misappropriating local government allocations. Adebutu made the remarks at the Ake Palace in Abeokuta during a meeting with the Egba Tra

item.title: Obidient Movement rallies support for Peter Obi’s 2027 presidential bid
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He criticised what he described as wasteful public expenditure and called for prudent management of national resources to improve education, healthcare, infrastructure and security.

item.title: Dozens missing after passenger ferry sinks off coast of Guyana
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Emergency response has rescued 67 people, including 15 children, from one of the country’s worst maritime disastersA ferry carrying 116 passengers and crew sank ⁠off the coast of Guyana in South America ⁠late Saturday, with 67 ⁠people, ​including 15 children, rescued so far, according to Guyana ⁠aut

item.title: Group Urges Tinubu, APC Chair To Intervene As Stakeholders In Niger Seek Unity
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …As ex-commissioners, lawmakers, party leaders finalise mass defection plans President Bola Tinubu and the National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress, Professor Nentawe Yilwatda, have been urged to urgently intervene to restore unity in the Niger State chapter of the party, amid growin

item.title: Alake, NIMC join forces to track illegal miners, boost digital governance
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has intensified efforts to combat illegal mining and strengthen governance in the solid minerals sector through a strategic partnership between the Ministry of Solid Minerals Development and the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC). The post Alake, NIMC join forces t

item.title: INEC portal: PDP, LP assure of manpower to upload guber, Assembly candidates
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Amid concerns over the capacity of political parties to upload candidates' lists to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal, The post INEC portal: PDP, LP assure of manpower to upload guber, Assembly candidates appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: NDLEA intercepts tramadol shipments bound for Kano, arrests 80-year-old grandfather
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has recorded fresh successes in its ongoing crackdown on a transnational drug trafficking syndicate smuggling tramadol from Togo through the Benin Republic into Nigeria, with the interception of two trailers concealing a combined 1,630,000 pills of tr

item.title: Trump arrives at stadium for World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: US President Donald Trump arrived on Sunday for the World Cup final at the MetLife Stadium near New York where Argentina will take on Spain at the climax of the 48-team tournament. The post Trump arrives at stadium for World Cup final appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Modder Runs GTA III Inside GTA: San Andreas on an In-Game TV
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://videocardz.com/newz/modder-runs-gta-iii-inside-gta-san-andreas-on-an-in-game-tv Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48970991 Points: 37 # Comments: 9

item.title: Kodak EC35 is a dirt-cheap point-and-shoot film camera
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Following the success of its $99 Kodak-branded Snapic A1, Reto Project is releasing the Kodak EC35, an even more affordable 35mm film camera for just $34.99. The EC35 certainly isn't fancy. Its 25mm acrylic lens with a fixed f/10 aperture and 1/100 shutter speed basically put it on par with a drugst

item.title: England clinch India series in Lord's run-fest
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Ben Duckett's century and Sam Curran's late burst of wickets inspired England to a series-clinching 27-run win over India in the third ODI at Lord's.
